description: secretx-env/src/lib.rs:123,126 The #[cfg(unix)] non-UTF-8 test calls std::env::set_var and std::env::remove_var WITHOUT an unsafe block. Under edition 2024 these are unsafe fn and the call will fail to compile. The workspace Cargo.toml currently pins edition = "2021" so this compiles today, but every OTHER crate in this workspace that touches set_var/remove_var (secretx-systemd, secretx-gcp-sm, secretx-bitwarden, secretx-pkcs11) already wraps them in unsafe { }. secretx-env is the lone outlier and will be the one site that breaks at the next edition bump. Verified: 23 matches across the workspace, 21 already use unsafe; only the 2 sites in secretx-env do not. Fix (minimum): wrap both calls in unsafe { } to match the workspace convention and forward-port for edition 2024. Fix (better): consider whether driving this branch through OsString fabrication without writing to the live process environment is feasible, since the std docs warn that set_var races with any concurrent getenv anywhere in the process — even with unsafe, this remains a thread-safety hazard that the test framework can hit when other tests in the same binary read env vars (tests get_present_var and refresh_rereads in this same module read PATH).
title: Test calls env::set_var/remove_var without unsafe — outlier in workspace
